w:r.w:t: I have created this document to help reduce costs without compromising quality. Please review it carefully and check what has already been implemented and what is still missing. Execute all pending items exactly as mentioned in the document.
3.5 Search & Nearby — Combined Elasticsearch + PostGIS Pipeline
3.11 OpenStreetMap Tile Fair-Use Policy (Mobile Fallback Only)
Web: Google Maps Embed API on business profile pages — free, unlimited.
Mobile: Google Maps SDK — free, unlimited.
No map auto-loads. Default view is always list view. Map loads only when user clicks "View on Map".
Mobile fallback: if SDK fails, show a static
tile + address text + Retry button (one try/catch in Flutter — no full OSM integration). See Section 3.11 for OSM fair-use rules.
Device GPS only — no external API. Accuracy: within 100 m.
If GPS denied or unavailable: Manual city selection → Manual pin placement.
Never request location at app launch.
Never store GPS location beyond the active session.
Use Google Places Autocomplete. Trigger after 3+ characters with 300
Session tokens are mandatory — without them every keystroke is a separate billable API call.
. Do not store any raw Google Places data.
Never geocode on the frontend. Use
Before calling the API, check if the same address already exists in DB — reuse stored coordinates if found.
Retry failed geocoding up to 3 times. Send admin alert if all retries fail.
worker concurrency to maximum 10 concurrent jobs, with a minimum 100
delay between jobs. This prevents hitting Google's QPS limit during registration spikes.
handles geo-boundary filtering. Both are mandatory and must work together in every search request. Never use one without the other for business discovery queries.
Every business discovery search must follow this exact pipeline:
Receives query text + category + filters. Scores and ranks matching business documents by relevance. Returns a list of matching business IDs with scores.
Takes the business IDs from Step 1. Applies the user's chosen radius filter (1 km, 5 km, 10 km, 25 km,
km). Eliminates results outside the boundary.
relevance score + distance. Applies final sort (relevance, distance, or rating as chosen by user). Returns paginated result set.
Caches the final merged result set keyed by search parameters. TTL: 15 minutes. See Section 3.6 for full cache rules.
Index name: businesses. Map the following fields: name (text, analyzed), category (keyword), tags / keywords (text), city (keyword), status (keyword),
Use a custom analyzer for Urdu/Roman Urdu transliteration where applicable.
Always filter by status: active before returning results — never surface pending or suspended listings.
Boost fields in order: exact name match > category match > keyword match > description match.
(not from/size) for deep pagination at scale.
on the geography type (not geometry) for accurate
Add a GIST spatial index on every location column: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY
Radius options available to user: 1 km, 5 km, 10 km, 25 km, 50 km.
on the full table without an ID filter.
Search API, Distance Matrix API, or Directions API.

FIX APPLIED v2.4: Full cache invalidation rules added. Missing invalidation causes stale results — closed businesses shown as open, deleted listings appearing in search. This is a trust-destroying bug. Follow all rules below without exception.
All search result cache keys must follow this exact format:
is an MD5 or SHA-1 of the normalized query string + active filters.
On business address update: immediately delete all cache keys matching
SCAN + DEL — never KEYS in production.
On business status change (activation, suspension, deletion): immediately purge all cache keys that could contain this business. Fire a
cache-invalidation job on every status transition.
On business category update: purge cache keys for both old and new categories in the affected city.
On business deletion: purge business profile cache key (
{id}) AND all city-level search caches where it appeared.
On business hours update (open/closed status change): purge the business profile cache immediately. Search caches with "Open Now" filter must be purged within 1 minute.
Never use FLUSHDB or FLUSHALL for targeted invalidation — these wipe the entire cache.
After any bulk invalidation event, queue a
cache-warm job for the top 20 most-searched city/category/radius combinations.
Warm jobs run at off-peak times (02:00–05:00 local) to repopulate expired popular searches.
Add 'Open in Google Maps' button on every business profile.
No in-app navigation. No Routes API. No Directions API.
Flag as duplicate only when 2 or more of these signals match simultaneously:
Within 10 m of an existing listing
. Normalize before comparison: lowercase, trim spaces, strip punctuation, normalize Unicode, apply transliteration.
Exact match — normalize to E.164 format before comparison.
1 signal match = soft warning, user can continue.
2+ signals = confirmation prompt shown to user.
Never hard-block automatically — always allow user override.
Validate on both frontend and backend.
is already available in PostgreSQL with
setups. It is free, fast, and handles fuzzy text matching natively — no extra library needed. Enable with: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS
Never call Google API clients directly in application code.
Wrap all map/location API calls in three
The rest of the app calls only these services. Switching providers later requires changing only these files.
— this is the single entry point for all combined
search queries. No other layer calls
Set hard daily quota limits for all paid APIs in Google Cloud Console.
Set billing alerts at 50% and 90% of monthly budget.

Important: Business profile pages must remain publicly accessible (no login required to view them). The Google Maps Embed API is free for public pages. Putting it behind a login wall may violate Google's Terms of Service.
NEW in v2.4: OSM tile fair-use rules added. Violating these terms can result in your a
pp being IP-blocked by OSM tile servers — causing the fallback to fail for all users.

The OSM static tile fallback is used only in the mobile app, only when the Google Maps SDK fails, and only for a single static tile per failure event. Despite this limited use, the following rules apply:
Use tile.openstreetmap.org for MVP only. If monthly active users exceed 50,000, migrate to a self-hosted tile server or a CDN-backed provider (e.g., Stadia Maps free tier,
The OSM Tile Usage Policy prohibits bulk or automated tile fetching. This fallback fires only on SDK failure — one tile per event. This is compliant.
Cache returned OSM tiles on the device for a minimum of 24 hours. Never re-fetch the same tile within 24 hours.
contributors" text on any screen showing an OSM tile. This is legally mandatory under the
At 50,000+ MAU: migrate OSM tiles to Stadia Maps free tier (no key required up to 200,000 tile requests/month) or self-hosted. Document this in post-launch roadmap.
These figures assume full compliance with this specification: Embed API on web, Maps SDK on mobile,
on self-hosted AWS (included in infra cost),
Search, Distance Matrix, or Directions API usage.
Google Maps Embed API used on web profile pages
Google Maps SDK used in mobile app
No map auto-loads — user must click to load
OSM static tile fallback implemented in Flutter (try/catch only)
OSM tiles cached on device for minimum 24 hours
contributors" attribution displayed on OSM tile screens
OSM migration plan documented for 50,000+ MAU milestone
Device GPS used for user location — no external API
Location not requested at app launch, not stored beyond session
Session tokens implemented and verified working
stored — no raw Google Places data
Address deduplication check runs before geocoding API call
Geocoding retries up to 3 times, admin alert on full failure
index created with correct field mappings
always filters status: active before returning results
used on geography type for all radius filtering
GIST spatial index created on all location columns
is the single entry point for all search queries
TTL: 15 min for search results, 30 min for profiles
Cache invalidation fires on: address update, status change, category change, deletion
cache-invalidation job fires on every business status transition
KEYS command is never used in production
Cache warming job scheduled for off-peak hours on top search combos
for name/address, E.164 for phone, 2+ signals required
Names/addresses normalized (lowercase, stripped punctuation) before similarity check
calls in application code — services only
Hard daily quota limits set in Google Cloud Console
Billing alerts set at 50% and 90% of budget
All banned APIs confirmed disabled in Google Cloud project
Business profile pages are publicly accessible (no login required to view)
